name: Damon Wayans
photo_credit: Brian Solis via Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 2.0)
blurb: The middle Wayans who almost got it both ways — fired from SNL for going too gay, then turned around and built In Living Color into a Black-comedy machine the networks had no answer to.
bio: Damon Wayans started stand-up in 1982 and got his first SNL chair from 1985–86, where Lorne Michaels fired him after 11 episodes for improvising a flamboyantly gay cop instead of the straight one written into the sketch — a story Wayans tells as the moment he learned to write for his own room instead of someone else's. He spent the late 1980s in the Comedy Store / one-night-stand club ecosystem and on HBO's One Night Stand (1989), then co-created In Living Color with his brother Keenen on Fox in 1990 — the show invented Homey D. Clown, Handi-Man, and the language a whole generation of Black sketch comedy would speak. Wayans cut his last special in 1990 (HBO's The Last Stand?), toured stand-up across the My Wife and Kids and Lethal Weapon TV years, and formally retired from stand-up at the Irvine Improv in December 2015 after a pituitary surgery. The Comedy Store club years and Last Stand? are the stand-up record worth ranking; the In Living Color characters are the next page over.

timeline.ev: Born September 4 in Harlem, New York City
Begins stand-up at New York / LA clubs
Brief film debut as the 'Banana Man' in Beverly Hills Cop
SNL featured player (Season 11); fired after 11 episodes for improvising a sketch
HBO One Night Stand — Damon Wayans episode
HBO special The Last Stand?; co-creates In Living Color on Fox with brother Keenen
The Last Boy Scout (with Bruce Willis) — first major leading-man film role
My Wife and Kids premieres on ABC; runs five seasons
Announces stand-up retirement at the Irvine Improv (December 2015); undergoes brain surgery for a benign pituitary tumor
Returns to scripted TV with son Damon Jr. on CBS sitcom Poppa's House

accolades: HBO comedy special The Last Stand? (1990)
Co-creator of In Living Color (Fox, 1990–1994) — three Emmy writing/performance nominations
NAACP Image Award winner for Outstanding Actor in a Comedy Series (My Wife and Kids, 2024 cycle for Poppa's House)
Member of the Wayans family of entertainers (eldest comedy generation of brothers and sister)

movies_tv.role: Co-creator / cast (Homey D. Clown, Handi-Man, others)
